Ad Text:
Overview

A large, complex enterprise organisation is seeking a hands-on Data Analytics Lead to take ownership of its central data platform and support the ongoing development of its data capability.

This role sits across data engineering, data modelling, and business engagement. The focus is on building out a centralised data warehouse and developing domain-level data models that support decision-making across a highly operational, multi-domain business.

Role Summary

This is not a traditional BI or pure data engineering role.

You will act as the bridge between business and data, working closely with subject matter experts to understand how different parts of the organisation operate, and translating that into structured, scalable data models within the enterprise data platform.

The role requires both hands-on technical capability and the ability to operate in ambiguous, stakeholder-heavy environments.

Key Responsibilities

Data Modelling & Platform Development

Design and build integrated data models across multiple business domains

Develop semantic data layers that provide a consistent, business-facing view of data

Consolidate data from multiple source systems into a central platform

Contribute hands-on to complex data modelling and integration challenges

Business Engagement & Data Definition

Work with business SMEs to:

Understand operational processes

Define metrics, KPIs, and data definitions

Drive alignment and standardisation across domains

Translate business concepts into structured data models

Data Governance & Domain Alignment

Lead working groups to validate and agree data definitions

Support governance processes to ensure consistency and sign-off

Build a strong understanding of how data is used across the business

Analytics Enablement

Enable analytics that support operational and commercial outcomes

Provide data structures that underpin reporting, forecasting, and optimisation

Support broader transformation initiatives through data

Team Coordination

Work with a small team of onshore and offshore data engineers

Provide technical guidance while remaining hands-on

Technical Environment

Centralised enterprise data warehouse / data lake architecture

Multiple upstream operational systems feeding into a unified platform

Domain-based data modelling with semantic layers

Power BI (or similar) for reporting and visualisation

The platform is designed to ingest and standardise business-critical data, providing a foundation for analytics and future AI use cases.

Key Requirements

Strong experience in data engineering and/or data warehousing environments

Proven capability in:

Data modelling (dimensional, semantic, or domain-driven)

Data integration across multiple systems

Advanced SQL skills and strong understanding of data structures

Experience working in complex, multi-domain environments

Ability to operate across both technical delivery and business engagement

Consulting-style experience is highly regarded, including:

Working with business stakeholders to define requirements

Structuring ambiguous problems

Translating business processes into data models

Experience leading or coordinating small teams

Comfortable remaining hands-on with technical delivery

Key Objectives

Build a consistent and integrated view of data across the organisation

Standardise metrics and definitions across business domains

Improve how the business understands and uses data

Support the development of a scalable data foundation for advanced analytics and AI

Logistics

Location: Sydney (hybrid working model)

Contract role (long-term engagement)

Immediate start preferred

This role will best fit someone who has led small to medium-sized digital or data transformations within large organisations. It is not a 'hands-on' technical role; it is an enablement and engagement position.

About the Job 

Due to the continued success and growth of Seven Consulting, we are committed to building a Change Management Practice to complement our Program Delivery, Program Management Office (PMO) and Consulting services.  

Established in 2002, Seven Consulting is Australia's best program delivery company. Our results show client satisfaction at 99.20% and team satisfaction at 98.01% for 2022, and those figures continue to hold firm. 

We specialise in the successful delivery of large-scale, complex IT-enabled business transformation projects ranging from $1M to $1.4Bn with some of Australia's top companies. We have offices in Sydney, Melbourne and Manila. 

We are looking for talented Change Analysts/Associates with relevant experience, who share our passion for delivering quality outcomes through business change, to join our team on a full-time basis. 

 

Your New Role 

As a Change Analyst/Associate, you will work directly with our clients across all areas of Change Management to deliver high quality outcomes. As this is a junior role, you will work closely with more senior team members on client engagements. Each engagement is different, but typical responsibilities of a Change Analyst/Associate include: 

Analysing a client's business particularly where there is a potential impact from change 

Meeting and presenting to stakeholders including preparing business documents and slides  

Proposing appropriate tactics to manage organisational changes for a project in a way that leads to the desired outcomes 

Executing on plans, scheduling, reporting on progress, raising risks and issues, maintaining effective relationships, and being a supportive project team member 

Applying past experience in projects to demonstrate knowledge of Project Management principles as well as Agile, Waterfall and blended methods 

Providing the needed level of support to the recipients of change during and after the project 

 

What You Bring 

Coming into the Change Management Practice at the Change Analyst/Associate level means that you will bring professional experience in consulting and/or knowledge of project delivery and exposure to delivery of organisational change. Typically, Change Analysts/Associates have: 

1 to 2 years of experience in Change Management, Project Delivery and/or PMO roles 

A relevant certification or a Bachelor's degree (often in Psychology and Organisational Psychology, Communications/Journalism, Human Resources, Project Management, Applied / Behavioural Science or similar) 

Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite, especially Word and PowerPoint  

Strong written and verbal communication skills 

A desire and ability to contribute to the Change Management Practice




You will also possess the following essential attributes for managing change at this level 

Resilience 

An inquisitive nature 

Empathy  

Problem analysis and solving 

To be eligible for employment you must either be an Australian citizen or permanent resident or have a visa with work rights. 

 

What We Offer 

Seven Consulting is committed to building a community of high calibre Program Delivery, PMO, Change Management and Consulting professionals and providing a rewarding career by offering: 

A continuous learning and development environment 

In-house training sessions and external training allowances 

Individual career development plans  

A mentoring / mentee program 

A collaborative and inclusive culture 

Excellent remuneration, regular and well-defined bonuses and profit share 

Paid overtime 

Recognition and rewards for high performance 

Job security and support during personal hardships 

A team charity initiative – Project 7 – to support the community 

Quarterly social and family events 

Please visit our website to learn more careers and culture at Seven: https://www.sevenconsulting.com/ 

 

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion 

Seven Consulting embraces diversity and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin or on the basis of disability. 

Seven Consulting is committed to providing a safe environment for our team members. All applicants are required to provide any relevant checks and COVID immunisation certificates. 

This is an excellent opportunity to join Seven and be part of our continued growth and success. If you are looking for an organisation that values your experience and expertise, then apply now to be involved in some of the industry’s most significant projects. 

Please apply online or contact our HR team on (03) 9617 8200 for a confidential discussion.

We are seeking experienced Data Operations professionals to join a specialist Data Ops function supporting a major transformation program within a financial services environment. These roles will play a critical part in SIT and UAT readiness, ensuring data operability, integrity, and successful transition toward future production operations.
This is an opportunity to work in a high-impact, complex data environment, validating critical data flows between systems and building the operational foundations for long-term BAU capability.
About the Role You will operate within a small, high-performing Data Ops team responsible for validating and running data operations in a test environment (SIT/UAT). Working closely with Environment, Test teams, and Business SMEs, you will ensure data is complete, accurate, and operationally ready.
Depending on experience, you may step into one of the following capacities:

Data Operations Lead – Owning SIT/UAT data operability and readiness, acting as the primary interface across teams
Senior Data Analyst / Data SME – Deep-diving into data lineage, defects, and outcomes
Operations Analyst – Supporting monitoring, controls, and documentation to ensure consistency and reduce key person dependency
There is strong potential for extension into future production data operations or continued involvement across SIT/UAT cycles.
Key Responsibilities
Run and monitor batch jobs, interfaces, and data files across SIT and UAT environments
Validate job sequencing, dependencies, and end-to-end data flows
Identify, analyse, and triage data and operational defects
Support defect resolution through detailed analysis and evidence-based insights
Build and maintain runbooks, data flow diagrams, and recovery procedures
Develop and maintain the Master Interface Inventory (MII)
Confirm critical data flows between systems (e.g. Pepper and UMP) are complete, accurate, and fit for purpose
Define and validate data critical to operational processing and reporting
Collaborate with Environment, Test teams, and SMEs to ensure readiness for production transition
Key Deliverables
Design: Master Interface Inventory (MII) including ongoing maintenance
Test: SIT/UAT job sequencing validation, defect analysis, and root cause documentation
Operations: Data flow and dependency mapping, evidence of operational readiness
About You We are looking for candidates with a blend of data analysis, operations, and financial services experience, ideally within core banking or mortgage environments.
You will bring:

Strong experience in data operations, data analysis, or PMO/data support roles
Proven ability to analyse data flows, lineage, and defects in complex environments
Experience working across SIT/UAT testing phases
Understanding of batch processing, interfaces, and system dependencies
Ability to create clear documentation, runbooks, and operational artefacts
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across technical and business teams
Background in financial services (highly regarded)
For more information, please email peter@employ.com.au

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - ICT Transformation Program
Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst - ICT Transformation Program NV1

Location: Canberra, Sydney or Melbourne
Security Clearance: NV1 required
Employment Type: Contract

An opportunity is available for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join a large-scale, mission-critical ICT transformation program within a secure government environment.

This program is focused on modernising legacy systems and integrating hybrid, multi-cloud infrastructure into a unified, secure ecosystem. The initiative aims to improve observability, operational performance, cyber resilience and overall user experience, while adopting best-in-class platforms and reducing technical debt.

Key Responsibilities
Lead business analysis activities across enterprise ICT uplift initiatives, transitioning on-premise systems to secure cloud environments
Develop and deliver high-quality artefacts, including:
Business Case inputs (options, risks, benefits, outcomes)
Operational Concept Documents (OCD)
Functional and Performance Specifications (FPS)
Verification Cross Reference Matrices (VCRM)
Define and implement business analysis approaches, governance frameworks and quality standards
Facilitate requirements workshops with diverse stakeholder groups and competing priorities
Lead process modelling, persona development and user journey mapping
Ensure alignment of requirements and benefits with program objectives
Manage end-to-end requirements lifecycle including traceability, validation, approvals and change control
Oversee non-functional requirements including security, resilience, interoperability and performance
Collaborate closely with Architecture, Engineering, Security, Testing and IT Service Management teams
Required Skills & Experience
Demonstrated experience as a Senior Business Analyst in complex ICT transformation programs
Strong experience transitioning enterprise environments from on-premise to hybrid/multi-cloud platforms
Proven ability to produce structured, high-quality artefacts (OCD, FPS, VCRM, Business Case inputs)
Expertise in requirements governance, traceability and quality assurance processes
Advanced stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills
Strong understanding of non-functional requirements and validation approaches
Experience working across cross-functional teams including Architecture, Engineering, Security and ITSM
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Ability to operate in high-security, mission-critical environments
Desirable
Experience with Azure and/or AWS in hybrid/multi-cloud environments
Knowledge of zero-trust security, IAM, observability and monitoring platforms
Familiarity with ITIL/ITSM practices (service design, transition, BAU acceptance)
Experience with ServiceNow
Additional Information
Candidates must hold an active NV1 security clearance
Roles available across Canberra, Sydney or Melbourne
Work on a high-impact program delivering critical national capability

The role
This is a hands-on delivery role, in addition to governance. You'll own execution across three parallel streams, managing wave-based migrations, a delivery partner, and a diverse mix of academic and professional stakeholders.

Drive wave-based migrations across multiple concurrent business units
Manage end-to-end delivery: data review, migration, post-migration support
Oversee legacy data remediation and information governance uplift
Manage the external delivery partner, dedicated internal project team, and coordinate internal technical teams where needed
Navigate risk, dependencies, and competing priorities at scale
Maintain momentum across a high-visibility, politically complex environment
 
What you'll bring

Exceptional stakeholder management across diverse, competing groups
Proven delivery of large-scale M365 or enterprise data migration programs
Strong working knowledge of SharePoint, Teams, and cloud migration environments
Experience with legacy data, remediation, and information management principles
Ability to operate across strategic and operational delivery levels simultaneously
Confidence managing delivery partners in complex governance settings
 
What will set you apart

Wave-based or multi-stream migration experience
Track record stepping into in-flight programs and maintaining momentum
Strong judgement navigating variability across a large stakeholder base
 
 
Key Responsibilities

Maintain overall accountability for planning, coordinating, and delivering the Project within agreed scope, time, cost, and quality tolerances.
Ensure all project outcomes strictly align with approved initiative proposals and DBS governance expectations.
Manage the project management framework, including project plans, schedules, resourcing models, and governance cadence in accordance with DBS delivery standards.
Drive stakeholder engagement by collaborating closely with business representatives to ensure strategic alignment and facilitate timely decision-making.
Manage vendors and external partners, providing oversight of contracted services, deliverables, milestones, and performance against agreed Statements of Work (SoW).
Lead risk, issue, and dependency management by proactively identifying, assessing, and mitigating threats, ensuring clear treatment plans are established and escalated appropriately.
Provide comprehensive financial and benefits oversight, including tracking expenditure, forecasting, and supporting the realisation of agreed operational benefits.
Manage any vendor engagements, such as strategic procurement of services required to deliver the projects objectives.
Coordinate change and adoption initiatives to align technical delivery with user readiness, training, and transition activities.
Deliver regular, accurate project reporting to sponsors and governance forums, detailing project status, risks, issues, financials, and upcoming milestones.
Manage the seamless transition to operations across all teams, ensuring all completed deliverables are fully documented, formally accepted, and handed over to steady-state support with robust operational models in place.
 
Key Skills

Exceptional ability to build relationships, negotiate, and drive consensus across diverse technical teams, business units, and executive leadership in a highly matrixed environment.
Strong skills in guiding business units through the cultural and operational shifts associated with process design and adoption, ensuring high user readiness and smooth transitions.
Proven capability to lead, motivate, and align multidisciplinary teams, including process and business analysts, technical specialists and operational staff without relying on direct reporting lines.
Expertise in overseeing ongoing vendor performance to ensure alignment with project goals and deliverables.
Adept at proactively identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks, technical roadblocks, and cross-team dependencies specific to the deployment of emerging technologies and enterprise-wide process changes.
Proven experience in delivering business requirements aligned to priorities and stakeholder expectations, with the ability to maintain continued alignment throughout delivery through ongoing confirmation at each transition.
Highly articulate in both written and verbal communication, capable of tailoring messaging to suit both highly technical audiences and non-technical business stakeholders.

An exciting opportunity is available for a Technical Product Owner to take ownership of a live, high-impact digital product and drive its next phase of growth.

This role sits within a forward-thinking digital team focused on data-driven decisioning, automation, and intelligent systems, working on solutions that directly influence operational performance in complex environments.

The Opportunity

You'll step into a product that is already in production and delivering value, with a clear mandate to scale, enhance, and evolve it through new features, POCs, and innovation initiatives.

The product operates in the digital decisioning and processing space, leveraging data and advanced analytics to optimise real-world processes.

You'll work closely with a cross-functional team of engineers, data specialists, and architects (7 direct technical contributors), acting as the critical link between business needs and technical delivery.

What You'll Be Doing

Own and drive the product roadmap, balancing operational stability with innovation and growth
Translate business problems into clear technical requirements and product features
Partner with engineers and data teams to deliver scalable, user-centric solutions
Lead backlog management, sprint planning, and prioritisation in an agile environment
Define and support system integration strategies across digital platforms
Use data insights to inform decisions, measure impact, and continuously improve the product
Oversee testing, validation, and release processes, ensuring smooth transition to production
Act as the voice of the user, ensuring solutions are practical, valuable, and intuitive

Tech & Product Environment

Focus: Digital decision systems, process optimisation
Data & Tools: SQL, Python, Power BI
Engineering Stack Exposure: C#, cloud platforms, integrated systems
Ways of Working: Agile delivery, continuous improvement, data-driven decision making

What We're Looking For

Proven experience as a Technical Product Owner, Product Manager, or Technical Business Analyst
Strong ability to bridge business context and technical execution
Experience owning products through delivery, release, and ongoing enhancement
Comfortable working with engineering, data, and architecture teams
Strong understanding of Agile and SDLC principles
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and drive decisions
Confident communicator who can influence stakeholders and align teams

Nice to Have

Background in data platforms, analytics, or AI-driven products
Experience working with industrial, operational, or asset-heavy environments
Exposure to real-time data, sensor-driven systems, or process optimisation

Why This Role

Take ownership of a live product with real-world impact
Shape the future direction through innovation, POCs, and new capabilities
Work at the intersection of data, engineering, and business strategy
Join a collaborative, high-performing team with strong technical depth
Flexible working and a strong focus on continuous improvement and outcomes

The Bottom Line

This role suits a technically minded Product Owner who thrives on taking products beyond launch-someone who can connect the dots between data, systems, and user needs, and drive meaningful outcomes in complex environments.

To express your interest, reach out via email on adam.dunn@talenza.com.au - Your interest will be treated in the strictest of confidence.

An exciting opportunity awaits for you to join a forward-thinking government department in Brisbane as a Senior Data Engineer. This role places you at the heart of a transformative data modernisation programme, where your expertise will directly influence the development of an enterprise data platform supporting large-scale ICT transformation.

Roles and Responsibilities

Design, implement, and maintain robust, scalable, and secure data pipelines and architectures using Microsoft Fabric and related tools within hybrid cloud environments.
Manage complex data ingestion, transformation, and storage processes in Fabric Lakehouses or Delta Lakes by utilising PySpark, PySQL, and delta format to optimise availability for analytics.
Apply advanced SQL development techniques for database management, integration tasks, query optimisation, and performance tuning across multiple platforms.
Monitor and troubleshoot data pipelines proactively to ensure high levels of availability, reliability, and system performance for critical business operations.
Implement comprehensive data quality management practices to identify issues early, resolve them efficiently, and ensure compliance with governance standards.
Prepare and normalise large datasets to support statistical analysis and machine learning models that enable advanced analytics and informed decision-making.
Identify opportunities to enhance analytical capabilities by optimising existing models and improving workflows for greater efficiency.
Provide mentorship and training to colleagues on best practices in data engineering tools, techniques, and methodologies.
Create detailed technical documentation covering architecture designs, operational processes, troubleshooting guides, and user access management protocols.
Support DevOps practices such as continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) to streamline solution deployments while enhancing platform reliability.

The ideal Candidate

Demonstrated experience of at least three years in data engineering roles involving Azure Synapse, complex data pipelines, lakehouse architectures, and SQL-based tools such as SSIS or SSRS.
Proven ability to design scalable solutions within hybrid cloud environments using advanced preparation and transformation techniques tailored for large datasets.
Expertise in building robust pipelines for ingestion and transformation using Azure frameworks alongside proficiency with PySpark or PySQL.
Strong command of SQL development including advanced database management skills relevant to integration projects across diverse platforms.
Familiarity with preparing datasets for statistical analysis or machine learning applications through effective normalisation strategies.
Solid understanding of implementing rigorous data quality management processes that ensure reliable outcomes aligned with governance requirements.
Experience supporting business transitions into new analytics patterns-particularly those involving Microsoft Fabric-is highly regarded but not mandatory.
A collaborative approach when working with stakeholders or project teams during periods of change or transition between project phases.
Adaptability in responding positively to shifting operational demands within hybrid project-to-operations environments.
Possession of Microsoft Azure Data Engineer Associate certification or equivalent is highly desirable; additional certifications in SQL or related fields are considered advantageous.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply, please click "Apply" or contact Richard Lynch on 07 3032 2295 or richard.lynch@robertwalters.com.au for a confidential discussion.

Ad Text:
Senior Manager – Finance Business Partnering

About Public Skills Australia
Public Skills Australia is one of ten Jobs and Skills Councils appointed by the Australian Government, playing a key role in shaping the future workforce across the Public Safety and Government sectors.

Working across industries including police, fire and emergency services, corrections, defence and all levels of government, Public Skills Australia brings together employers, unions and government to develop workforce insights, inform national policy and support training and skills development.

Now three years into its journey, the organisation is entering its next phase of maturity, with a strong focus on embedding structure, strengthening delivery and continuing to elevate the impact of its work nationally.

About the Role
An initial 12 month fixed term contract Reporting to the Director, Workforce Strategy & Insights, the Manager, Workforce Planning & Insights is a key leadership role responsible for delivering Public Skills Australia’s workforce planning and research agenda.

Leading a specialist team, you will oversee the development of Workforce Insights Reports, the organisation’s core deliverable which inform national workforce planning, policy direction and funding priorities. This includes managing end to end delivery of complex, research led work programs, ensuring outputs are well planned, well consulted and delivered to a high standard.

Alongside this, you will oversee a portfolio of targeted research projects, responding to emerging workforce challenges and industry needs. This role requires strong project management capability, with the ability to manage multiple priorities, maintain momentum and ensure key milestones are met.
Leadership is central to success in this role. You will bring structure, clarity and accountability to a capable team, ensuring consistent delivery and high quality outputs in a fast paced environment.

Externally, you will engage with a diverse stakeholder group including government, industry and unions, contributing to consultation, workshops and strategic discussions. Internally, you will work closely with the Director and executive team, supporting broader organisational priorities and future planning.

This is a highly visible role suited to someone who enjoys operating at pace, navigating complexity and contributing to work that has national impact.

About You
You are an experienced leader with a background in workforce planning, policy, research or a related field, with the ability to translate data and consultation into meaningful insights.

You have experience delivering complex, research led programs of work and are comfortable working within government or government adjacent environments. You bring strong project management capability, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver quality outputs within structured timeframes.

You are a decisive leader who is confident setting expectations, holding people accountable and driving delivery, while building trust and engagement within your team. You are comfortable leading in environments where there is a need to embed structure, uplift capability and strengthen ways of working.

You bring strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to operate with credibility at a senior level. You are an effective communicator, with sound judgement, political awareness and the ability to navigate complex environments with confidence.

Above all, you are motivated by purpose led work and the opportunity to contribute to workforce outcomes at a national level.

Job Title: Senior Data Enablement Analyst-Medicare Mental Health Check In
Ad Text:
Real Impact at Scale -Your work will directly influence clinical and operational decision-making across a national health system - improving patient outcomes, service delivery, and performance at scale.
Own and Shape the Analytics Function-Move beyond reporting into true enablement - acting as a product owner for analytics assets, shaping KPI frameworks, and embedding data-driven decision-making across the organisation.
Work at the Intersection of Data, Strategy and Healthcare Transformation-Engage with senior stakeholders, influence strategy, and contribute to system-wide transformation within one of Australia's largest and most respected health organisations.


What if your next role helped transform how data drives care across Australia?
St Vincent's Health Australia is building a future where data is not just reported - but actively shapes decisions, improves patient outcomes, and transforms how care is delivered.

We are now seeking a Senior Enablement Analyst to play a critical role in enabling data-driven decision-making across one of Australia's largest and most complex health organisations.

This is a rare opportunity to move beyond traditional reporting and step into a role where your insights directly influence clinical excellence, operational performance, and system-wide transformation.


About the Program - Medicare Mental Health Check-In
St Vincent's Health Australia has partnered with the Australian Government to deliver the Medicare Mental Health Check-In - a national digital mental health service designed to help Australians access care earlier and more easily.

This program brings together multiple national partners across technology, clinical workforce, digital innovation and lived experience to deliver a new model of accessible, scalable mental health support.

As part of this broader transformation, data and analytics play a critical role in enabling service performance, improving outcomes, and ensuring the program delivers meaningful impact at scale.


The Opportunity
As a Senior Enablement Analyst, you will sit at the intersection of business and technology - acting as the critical bridge between data, systems, and decision-making.

You will partner with clinical, operational, and corporate teams to bring data to life - translating complex information into meaningful insights that shape how performance is understood, measured, and improved.

Your work will ensure leaders have the clarity and confidence to make informed decisions that enhance care, improve efficiency, and support innovative, digitally enabled services - including national mental health initiatives.

This isn't just a reporting role - it's about storytelling, influence, and embedding a culture where data drives better outcomes.

This is a 12 month fixed term-full-time role based in Melbourne (hybrid), with the opportunity to influence decisions at both operational and executive levels.

What You'll Do

Translate complex data into compelling insights and stories that influence decision-making
Design and deliver intuitive dashboards and visualisations using tools like Power BI
Develop and maintain high-quality, governed datasets across multiple systems
Partner with stakeholders to define meaningful KPIs and performance measures
Act as a product owner for analytics assets, managing priorities and continuous improvements
Champion data quality, governance, and best-practice analytics across the organisation
Support and uplift data literacy, helping teams confidently use data in their day-to-day work

About You
You are a highly capable analytics professional who thrives in complexity and enjoys turning data into meaningful, actionable insight.

You're confident engaging with stakeholders at all levels - from executives to technical teams - and are comfortable challenging assumptions to ensure data quality, measurement integrity, and the right outcomes are achieved.


You Bring:

Senior Change Specialist - 6 month contract



Your New Company
You will be part of a large, complex global organisation undertaking a significant transformation of its project delivery capability. The program is implementing a new global project delivery system across three economic regions, supporting approximately 8,500 users worldwide and involving the migration of around 70,000 active projects.
In addition to the core system implementation, the broader program includes multiple initiatives such as enhanced project delivery learning, rostering improvements, and updates to enterprise‑wide standards, processes and ways of working. This is a fast‑paced, high‑impact transformation delivering lasting change across the organisation's global portfolio.


Your New Role
As Senior Change Specialist, you will play a key role in driving change and adoption activities for the rollout of the new project delivery system. Reporting to the Program Change Manager, you will focus on delivering tactical, practical change interventions that ensure stakeholders are informed, prepared and supported throughout each implementation release.
You will work closely with cross‑functional teams including learning, communications, content management and support teams to translate change impacts into actionable materials and ensure a smooth transition for end users before, during and after go‑live.


What You'll Need to Succeed
To be successful in this role, you will bring strong experience supporting large‑scale system implementations in complex, fast‑moving environments. You will demonstrate:

Proven experience in change management for large system or digital transformation programs, ideally within global or multi‑region environments
Strong capability in conducting and maintaining change impact assessments and translating impacts into clear actions
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to tailor messaging to different audiences
Experience working across cross‑functional delivery teams and supporting release‑based implementations
Familiarity with structured change methodologies such as Prosci / ADKAR (or equivalent)
Ability to create clear, concise communications and high‑quality visual presentations aligned to brand and corporate standards
Experience in consulting or advisory environments (highly advantageous)
Strong organisational skills and the ability to operate effectively in ambiguous, fast‑changing contexts
A proactive, solutions‑focused mindset with strong influencing and collaboration skills
What You'll Get in Return
In return, you will have the opportunity to contribute to a high‑profile global transformation program with meaningful business impact. You'll work alongside experienced change, delivery and leadership professionals in a collaborative and supportive environment, with exposure to large‑scale system implementation and enterprise change.


This is a 6‑month contract with a hybrid working model (three days per week in the CBD office and two days working from home). Some flexibility in working hours may be required to support stakeholders across different global time zones.


This role offers a flexible hybrid working model, strong visibility across the program, and the chance to apply your change expertise in a complex, global setting while delivering tangible outcomes for thousands of users worldwide.

The Data Governance Lead will be responsible for managing and maintaining the data governance framework, facilitating governance forums, and overseeing monitoring, reporting and continuous improvement of data governance activities and processes. This role involves developing, nurturing, and leveraging cross- business relationships to maximise business value of data governance.

Job Title: Group Procurement Operations, Systems & Analytics Manager
Ad Text:
The SunRice Group is an Australian ASX-listed branded food group, comprising multiple businesses, assets and operations across Australia, New Zealand, the Middle East, the United States, The Pacific Islands and Asia.  SunRice was built by the Australian rice industry over many decades and today is one of Australia’s leading branded food exporters, with approximately 2,000 employees and operations in 11 countries.

SunRice is a great place to work, providing a real opportunity for passionate people to make a difference.

 

About the role

We are looking for an experienced Procurement Operations, Systems & Analytics Manager to play a critical role in transforming a developing, global procurement function into a best‑in‑class operation.

This is a high‑impact role where you will help uplift procurement maturity by embedding robust processes, improving efficiencies, leveraging data and automation, and enabling the category management team to deliver maximum value through tendering and supplier strategies.

Reporting to the Group Procurement Director, you will lead procurement analytics, systems and operational enablement across a large, complex spend portfolio. This role is pivotal in moving procurement forward on their transformation journey - supporting stronger decision‑making, governance, sustainability outcomes, and financial performance across the business.

If you enjoy building, improving and modernising procurement functions - and know what “good” looks like in a mature environment - this role offers genuine scope to make a lasting impact.

 

Key responsibilities will include:

Driving the transformation and standardisation of procurement processes, tools and governance across global operations

Improving procurement efficiency and effectiveness through automation, better data quality and smarter use of systems

Developing and maintaining spend analytics, dashboards and reporting to support executive, board and finance requirements

Providing hands‑on operational support to category managers, including tender analysis, total cost modelling and sourcing insights to achieve best value outcomes

Enabling the category teams with clear data, tools and insights to improve sourcing decisions across contracts

Leading improvements across the source‑to‑pay lifecycle, including spend analytics, contract management and supplier performance reporting

Supporting the implementation and optimisation of procurement technologies and digital tools

Delivering procurement pipeline, savings, ROI and KPI reporting across the global function

Partnering with sustainability and ESG teams to support sustainable procurement reporting, including modern slavery and ESG requirements 

 

About You 

You are a commercially minded procurement professional who thrives in environments where there is opportunity to build, uplift and transform.

You bring experience from a more mature procurement function and can pragmatically apply best practice to a setting that is still evolving. You are confident working with data, comfortable influencing senior stakeholders, and motivated by the challenge of improving how things are done.

 

You will bring:

5–7+ years’ experience in procurement operations, analytics or enablement, ideally within a mature procurement function

Strong understanding of end‑to‑end procurement and tendering, with the ability to support category teams to deliver best‑value outcomes

Proven experience improving procurement processes, efficiencies and governance, including the use of data, automation and digital tools

Advanced analytical capability, with confidence creating dashboards, insights and executive‑ready reporting

Experience working with ERP, BI and e‑procurement platforms

Ability to clearly present insights and influence stakeholders across a global business

Exposure to sustainable procurement, ESG or supply chain sustainability reporting (beneficial)

A hands‑on, collaborative mindset and a genuine passion for transforming and uplifting procurement capability

 

What’s in it for you

At SunRice Group, we believe great work starts with feeling supported, valued and inspired. When you join our team, you’ll enjoy a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, growth and everyday life.

SunRice Product Allowance: Take home a taste of what we do best. You’ll receive a weekly allowance of SunRice Group products, so you can enjoy and share the food we’re proud to make.

Leave: Enjoy competitive leave benefits, including volunteer leave and the option to purchase additional leave when you need extra flexibility.

Wellness Reimbursement: Your wellbeing matters. Twice a year, you’ll be reimbursed for fitness memberships or other health and wellbeing initiatives that help you feel your best

 

All correspondence will be dealt with in strict confidence.

SunRice is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. SunRice will consider qualified applicants for employment based on their merit, individual skills, qualifications and other appropriate criteria relevant to the role for which they are applying.

A leading Australian multi-division group is undergoing a major finance transformation, building a modern, insight-led Commercial Finance function. This is a rare opportunity to step into a true build role, shaping Group FP&A, planning systems, and performance insight from the ground up. 

The role

The FP&A Manager will act as the central planning engine for the Group, owning budgeting, forecasting and long-range planning across a complex multi-entity environment. You will consolidate divisional inputs into a single Group view, ensuring consistency in assumptions, governance, and financial targets, while delivering high-quality insights to support executive and Board-level decision-making.

A key focus of the role is leading the transition from Excel-based planning to a system-enabled, driver-based planning environment, including implementation of a new Group planning and forecasting tool. You will partner closely with Finance Business Partners embedded across the business, who focus on commercial support, while FP&A sets frameworks, standards and Group performance visibility.

Key responsibilities include leading annual budgets and rolling forecasts, developing driver-based financial models, delivering executive reporting and variance analysis, establishing planning governance and cadence and supporting strategic modelling, scenario analysis and investment decisions. You will also lead implementation of the Group planning system, driving adoption, training, and change management across Finance and the broader business.

About you:

you are an experienced FP&A or Group Finance professional with strong budgeting, forecasting, and financial modelling capability. You will have exposure to finance transformation or planning system implementation, strong stakeholder engagement skills, and experience working in complex multi-entity or divisional organisations.
This is a foundational role where you will define and build Group FP&A capability, lead a major systems and process transformation, and help shift the finance function from reporting-focused to insight-led, commercially driven decision support.

Why This Role

This is a foundational FP&A leadership role where you will:

Build and define Group planning capability from the ground up
Lead a major finance systems and process transformation
Influence executive decision-making and Group strategy
Move the finance function from reporting-focused to insight-led
Play a key role in shaping a scalable, modern Commercial Finance function

Job Title: Senior Manager Employer Governance and Reporting
Ad Text:
About WorkCover Queensland

For over 20 years, WorkCover Queensland has been a self-funded, government-owned leader in the state’s workforce, providing empathetic and efficient workers’ compensation insurance. We are currently embarking on an enterprise-wide transformation guided by our 2030 Strategy. This is an exciting chapter focused on keeping Queenslanders working through innovation, integrity, and high-quality service delivery.

They are seeking a Senior Manager, Employer Governance & Reporting to play a pivotal role in enabling our Employer Group strategy through best-in-class data, governance and reporting capability.

About the Opportunity

Reporting to the GM Employer and sitting within the Employer Group Leadership Team, this role is central to ensuring the Account Management function is set up for success - with the right data, systems, processes and reporting frameworks in place.

This is a high-impact, build-and-shape role. You will co-design elements of the employer strategy while leading key initiatives such as CRM implementation, analytics uplift and the development of account management frameworks.

While there are no direct reports initially, there is clear scope to build and lead a team over time.
 
Key Responsibilities

Strategy, Governance & Enablement

Enable the Employer Group strategy and Engagement Plan through robust governance and reporting frameworks;
Co-design employer strategy, segmentation and account management frameworks; and
Lead governance processes, including incident management and audit support.
Data, Reporting & Insights

Establish trusted data, dashboards and reporting to drive performance and decision-making;
Deliver meaningful insights on employer trends, risks and opportunities; and
Lead the reporting cadence across the Employer Group.
Systems & Transformation

Own and lead CRM implementation and optimisation;
Partner with Technology to uplift systems, integrations and data capability; and
Drive analytics capability, including dashboarding (e.g. Power BI).
Capability & Stakeholder Engagement

Build governance and reporting capability through coaching, training and mentoring;
Partner closely with senior stakeholders, including the GM and ELT, providing clear and actionable insights; and
Support the Account Management function to lift performance through data-driven approaches.
About You

You’re a commercially minded and strategically focused leader who enjoys operating in greenfield or evolving environments, where you can build, influence and deliver tangible outcomes.

You will bring:

Strong experience in data governance, reporting and analytics leadership;
Proven capability in CRM development and/or optimisation;
Experience designing and governing enterprise-wide frameworks with commercial rigour;
Exposure to audit processes, governance and senior stakeholder briefing;
The ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights;
Experience in workers’ compensation is highly regarded; however, backgrounds in insurance or financial services (e.g. superannuation) will also be considered; and
Exposure to Power BI and experience building capability in others will be advantageous.

Elastic, the Search AI Company, enables everyone to find the answers they need in real time, using all their data, at scale — unleashing the potential of businesses and people. By taking advantage of all structured and unstructured data — securing and protecting private information more effectively — Elastic’s complete, cloud-based solutions for search, security, and observability help organizations deliver on the promise of AI.

What is The Role:


Elastic is looking for a driven, high-impact Inside Account Executive to accelerate both new business acquisition and expansion. This role partners closely with Account Executives to land new logos, grow customers, and drive meaningful revenue outcomes.

You’ll operate across the full sales cycle—from pipeline generation through to close—while helping unlock growth opportunities. If you’re passionate about technology, thrive in fast-paced environments, and want to help customers solve complex challenges through the power of search, we’d love to hear from you.

What You Will Be Doing:

Own your territory & build pipeline: Develop and execute a proactive outbound cadence (email, call, social) that generates at least 50% of your booked opportunities.
Deep discovery & qualification: Uncover pain, business impact, budget, and decision criteria using frameworks like MEDDPICC so you chase only the highest-confidence deals.
Value storytelling & demos: Craft and deliver tailored narratives and live demos that map Elastic’s Search, Observability, and Security capabilities to measurable business outcomes. Confidently present to both Technical and Executive audiences.
Mutual deal strategy & forecast accuracy: Collaborate with customers to build formal close plans and keep your CRM up-to-date, maintaining at least 90% forecast accuracy within ±10%.

Cross-functional partnership: Work hand-in-glove with Solutions Architects, Customer Success, Marketing, and RevOps to accelerate deals and drive exceptional customer outcomes.
What You Bring:

Proven SaaS quota‐carrying success: More than 3 years closing complex deals, consistently overachieving targets in a consumption-based or usage-model environment.
Expert discovery & qualification skills: Demonstrated ability to apply MEDDPICC or equivalent frameworks to drive disciplined pipeline and eliminate low-probability deals.
Compelling value storytellers: Track record of delivering executive-level presentations and demos that tie product capabilities to real dollars saved, revenue gained, or risk mitigated.
Strong negotiation chops: History of landing multi-year, high-ACV contracts while protecting margin and securing executive stakeholder buy-in.
Technical & cloud fluency: Comfortable discussing a broad range of technical topics including observability, security, vector/traditional search, and cloud cost optimization.
Collaborative mindset & coachability: A learner who partners effectively with internal teams, incorporates feedback, and embodies Elastic’s values of community and openness.
Open Source enthusiasm: Genuine appreciation for open-source communities and the Elastic model—bonus if you’ve sold or advocated in an OSS context.

Prior experience at an open-source or developer‐centric infrastructure company.
Familiarity with observability (logs, metrics, traces) or security analytics (SIEM/XDR) use cases.

Manager, Customer Insights

Clerk Grade: 11/12

Annual Salary Range: $149,739 - $173,174 plus superannuation

Employment Type: Ongoing, Full-time

Location: Sydney, McKell Building

About the team

In Customer Insights & Experience (CIE), our focus is ensuring the voices of people, communities, and businesses sit at the centre of decision-making and service delivery across NSW Government.

We help government understand what communities need, think, and experience - from detailed service interactions to broader community sentiment - and turn those insights into better decisions and better services.

You'll join a passionate team of specialists, with expertise in research, insights, customer experience, and service design. Together, we deliver actionable insights and practical improvements that shape policy, investment, communications, and service delivery across the whole of NSW Government - including departments, agencies, Ministers and Cabinet.

We generate actionable insights. Our insights specialists provide a deep understanding of the people, communities, and businesses to inform government decisions and identify opportunities to improve outcomes.
We turn insights into action. Our customer experience and service design specialists apply human-centered design, co-design, usability testing, and service transformation methods to create, refine, and uplift service experiences, ensuring tangible improvements for the people of NSW.

Your day-to-day

As a Manager, Customer Insights, you will play a lead role in the design, execution, and delivery of key research and insights projects - managing a team of research and insights specialists and working with key stakeholders to deliver actionable insights that inform government decision-making and service delivery.

This role requires a leader who can combine technical research expertise with strategic thinking, stakeholder engagement and team development. You will guide your team through rigorous research design, fieldwork, data analysis, insights development and presentations, ensuring insights are simple, compelling, and actionable.

In this role, you will:

Lead and manage a team of insights specialists, providing coaching and professional development to uplift capability and ensure consistent delivery of high-quality insights.
Oversee a portfolio of concurrent research and insights projects - including BAU reporting, whole-of-government insights programs, and bespoke research addressing agency-specific needs.
Proactively identify, leverage and integrate numerous datasets to develop insights reports with simple, clear, and actionable insights to stakeholders across all levels of government.
Engage with stakeholders across NSW Government to understand challenges, clarify objectives, scope work and translate needs into well-designed, costed insights proposals.
Lead the design, delivery, and execution of ad-hoc quantitative and qualitative insights projects to address key objectives and inform government decision-making, including research design, questionnaire design, discussion guide development, fieldwork or supplier management, data analysis, insights reporting, and presentations.
Deliver clear, actionable insights through reports, dashboards, presentations and workshops that support evidence-based decisions across policy, investment, communications, and service design and delivery.

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Extensive experience leading complex research and insights projects end-to-end, across qualitative and quantitative methods, sampling design, fieldwork, data analysis and insights communication.
Strong consulting capabilities - able to engage with senior stakeholders, translate ambiguous or high-level questions into structured research briefs, and design methodological approaches that deliver clear and actionable insights.
A proven ability to synthesise complex data into meaningful, actionable insights and communicate them effectively through written reports, visualisations, presentations and facilitated workshops.
Strong people leadership experience, including coaching, performance management, capability uplift and fostering a culture of excellence in insights and storytelling.
Strong project and portfolio management skills, with the ability to balance multiple priorities, maintain high quality standards and meet deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
Deep understanding of customer insights, community sentiment and customer experience measurement programs - ideally within large, complex or public sector contexts.
Highly developed interpersonal and influencing skills, with the ability to build trust, manage expectations and partner effectively across diverse teams and executive levels.
Experience with Q Research Software.
Strong proficiency in the Microsoft suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word).

What we need from you:

Company description:



About Newmont

Newmont is the world's leading gold company, producing gold as well as copper, silver, zinc, lead and molybdenum across operations in Australia, Papua New Guinea, Africa, Latin America and North America. In Australia, Newmont operates across Western Australia, the Northern Territory and New South Wales, supported by teams in Perth, Brisbane and Melbourne.

At Newmont, people create the value in the metals we mine and are ambassadors in the communities where they live and work. As a team, we foster an environment in support of our people vision, where everyone belongs, thrives and is valued. We have built a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can bring their differences to support delivering results together.

Join us and unearth your potential in a global company that is at the forefront of shaping the future of the mining industry.



Job description:



Your impact
Reporting to the Data Architecture Lead, you will support the development and evolution of Newmont's enterprise data platform by designing and delivering scalable data and AI solutions.

You will work with large, complex datasets to build and deploy modern data pipelines, machine learning and AI-enabled solutions that solve real business problems. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, data science and platform delivery, partnering closely with architecture, engineering and business teams to deliver secure, scalable and repeatable solutions.


As part of our team you will

Design, build and deploy data pipelines and AI/ML-enabled solutions using cloud-based platforms
Work with large structured and unstructured datasets to support enterprise data use cases
Translate solution design into executable technical delivery in an agile environment
Contribute to the standardisation of architecture and deployment patterns
Support the development of scalable, secure and repeatable data solutions
Apply and embed data governance, data quality and ethical AI practices
Collaborate with AI/ML, Digital Enablement and operational teams to solve business problems
Support end-to-end data workflows including ingestion, transformation and consumption
Optimise data processes and models for performance, scalability and reliability
Contribute to continuous improvement of the enterprise data platform and data capabilities


Profile description:



Prerequisites for your success in the role

Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
Strong SQL capability (essential), including experience working with large datasets
Experience with Python for data engineering or machine learning use cases
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Azure, AWS or similar)
Understanding of data governance, data quality and data management practices
Ability to translate business requirements into clear technical solutions
Experience working with enterprise-scale data or complex data environments

Well regarded:

Experience with Databricks or similar lakehouse platforms
Experience with large-scale data processing (e.g. Spark)
Exposure to machine learning frameworks (e.g. TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Experience with APIs and system integration in enterprise environments
Experience with GenAI, LLMs, or modern AI solution design (e.g. RAG, vector databases)
Experience in mining, resources, or heavy industry environments
Understanding of operational data and value chains

Your work environment
This role is ideally based in Perth, however flexible work arrangements, including remote options, will be considered in line with business and operational requirements.

You will work closely with a highly capable technical team across architecture, engineering and data functions, supporting stakeholders across operations and digital teams.

Occasional travel may be required to engage with stakeholders or support specific initiatives.

Join one of Australia's Big 4 Banks as an AI data Scientist in a new enterprise AI function of the bank. This is a rare opportunity to work at the forefront of applied AI while helping shape the next generation of banking technology.

These are some of the most sophisticated AI systems being built in financial services today. They demand cutting-edge thinking in agent architecture, evaluation methodology, and safety engineering, where reliability is critical and mistakes have real-world financial impact.

You'll work closely with the Head of Data Science and collaborate within small, highly technical squads where experimentation, rapid decision-making, and intellectual honesty are valued more than hierarchy or bureaucracy.

Key Responsibilities

Design and build agentic AI systems, including reasoning loops, orchestration logic, tool integrations, memory frameworks, and context management.
Translate AI architecture into production-ready solutions, bridging the gap between data science experimentation and scalable engineering implementation.
Develop prompting strategies and retrieval pipelines, while running rapid experimentation to improve agent performance and reliability.
Implement evaluation, monitoring, and safety frameworks to detect issues such as hallucinations, drift, or unexpected agent behaviour and ensure systems operate within defined guardrails.
Strengthen the broader AI capability by building reusable tools, sharing knowledge across teams, and mentoring junior data scientists.

Skills & Experience

Degree in a quantitative discipline such as computer science, machine learning, statistics, mathematics, physics, or engineering.
Proven experience delivering AI or machine learning systems into production environments.
Hands-on experience across machine learning and applied AI, with strong depth in areas such as NLP, recommender systems, ML modelling, or LLM-based applications.
Strong Python development skills and experience working with modern ML and AI ecosystems.
Practical experience across the ML lifecycle: data exploration, feature engineering, model development, evaluation, experimentation, and deployment.
Exposure to foundation model APIs, prompt engineering, embeddings, retrieval techniques, or contextual data pipelines.
Strong grounding in statistics, probability, and experimental design to rigorously evaluate AI models and systems.

To be eligible for this position, candidates must have full working rights in Australia.
